title: Granular Cloud
description: A grain-cloud synth — scatter a synthesized or recorded seed sound into shimmering, droney, or glitchy textures and render a WAV.
concept: Granular Cloud is a granular-synthesis instrument. Pick a synthesized seed (pad chord, filtered noise, Karplus-Strong pluck, vowel formant) or record 2 seconds from your mic, then scatter grains from it with live-tunable size, density, scan position, spray, pitch spread, reverse chance and reverb mix. A particle visualizer pulses with every grain, and the whole cloud can be rendered down to a real WAV file.
features: 4 synthesized seed sources plus live 2s mic recording
7 live-tunable grain parameters (size, density, position, spread, pitch, reverse, reverb)
draggable waveform scrubber to set scan position
4 built-in cloud presets (Shimmer, Drone, Chop, Reverse Rain) plus save/delete custom presets
particle cloud visualizer synced to grain spawns
6-second offline WAV render with one-click download

interaction.mouse: drag the waveform to set scan position; drag sliders to tune the grain cloud
interaction.click: tap seed-source chips, presets, Start/Stop, and Render WAV
interaction.touch: all controls are touch-friendly; waveform scrub and sliders work with a finger
interaction.keyboard: sliders are focusable and adjustable with arrow keys
